check: No hardcoded strings left in UI components
description: Grep for literal user-facing strings outside the message-file system before considering a locale-ready page done since component libraries and error boundaries are the most commonly missed spots
why_it_matters: A single hardcoded string breaks the fully-translated promise the moment that code path runs for a non-default locale

check: hreflang tags validated against the actual sitemap
description: Every hreflang alternate resolves to a real indexable URL that itself declares the reciprocal hreflang back validated with a crawler or the SEO module's technical checks rather than assumed correct
why_it_matters: Broken hreflang reciprocity is invisible in the UI but actively misdirects search engines to the wrong regional page

check: No IP-based or browser-only forced locale redirect
description: Locale is detected and offered never force-redirected without an override the user can change and that persists confirming a visitor can still reach another language version as an explicit choice
why_it_matters: Force-redirecting on IP or browser locale can hide entire locale versions from crawlers and trap users

check: Text expansion room verified for longer languages
description: German Finnish and Russian UI strings commonly run thirty to one hundred percent longer than English so buttons nav labels and form fields are tested with real translated content rather than English-length placeholders
why_it_matters: Layouts only tested in English silently break the moment a real locale ships

check: Plural and gender rules handled via ICU categories not conditionals
description: Verify translated strings use the library's plural or select syntax rather than a hardcoded two-way conditional that assumes English's plural rules
why_it_matters: Languages with more than two plural categories produce grammatically broken output under a naive English-shaped conditional

check: Dates numbers and currency rendered via the Intl API not manual string building
description: No hand-rolled date formatting or manual thousand-separator insertion anywhere in the codebase with Intl.DateTimeFormat and Intl.NumberFormat used universally
why_it_matters: Manually formatted dates and numbers are wrong by default outside the developer's own locale

check: RTL rendering tested for every RTL locale shipped
description: If Arabic Hebrew or Farsi ship the full page gets visual QA in RTL mode rather than assuming logical properties cascade correctly everywhere since custom components and third-party widgets are common holdouts
why_it_matters: Partial RTL support where text flips but icons or layout do not reads as broken rather than imperfect to native RTL readers

check: Missing translation keys degrade to the fallback locale never a raw key
description: Confirm the message library is configured to fall through the defined chain to a real language on a missing key since a raw key visible in production is a released bug not a translation gap
why_it_matters: Raw keys leaking into production UI are one of the most visible and embarrassing i18n failures and easy to prevent with fallback config

check: Translation coverage tracked per locale before launch
description: A simple coverage report showing the percentage of keys translated per locale generated from the message files or TMS is reviewed before adding a new locale to production routing
why_it_matters: Shipping a locale at partial coverage without knowing it is how raw keys and English fallback text end up live for paying users

check: Page titles meta descriptions and OG tags translated per locale
description: Confirm the title tag meta description and Open Graph tags are generated from the same localized content system as the page body rather than left in the default locale for every page
why_it_matters: Untranslated metadata is what shows in search results and social shares which is the exact surface a non-native-language visitor sees first

check: Pseudo-localization used in CI to catch untranslatable strings early
description: A pseudo-locale with accented and expanded placeholder text is run through the UI in CI or preview builds to catch concatenation truncation and hardcoded-string issues before real translations exist
why_it_matters: Catches structural i18n bugs automatically instead of waiting for the first real locale to surface them

check: Locale-specific legal and consent text reviewed by someone competent in that market
description: Terms privacy notices and consent banners for each shipped locale get a review pass by a native or fluent speaker with market context rather than machine translation alone
why_it_matters: A mistranslated consent banner can itself be a compliance failure not just an embarrassing one
